I am a Certified Backstage Associate (CBA) and have mentored three cohorts on Kubernetes, leading practical workshops and guiding professionals in effectively adopting this technology. Passionate about open source technologies, I explore and implement CNCF projects such as Kubernetes, Backstage, and others to drive innovation and operational efficiency, delivering scalable and high-impact solutions.

person.name: Nomeswaran Veligaram
person.bio: I am a Senior Software Engineer with expertise in Kubernetes and its ecosystem, complemented by strong development skills in Java and Go. I specialize in cloud-native technologies, container orchestration, and building scalable, resilient systems.

person.name: Rahul Jadhav
person.bio: An avid coder, a systems engineer working on solutions involving security and performance optimizations of cloud-native technologies. He runs a Kubernetes Cloud Native Security Bangalore Meetup and has been working closely with IETF Standards working groups. Taken several projects from conception to market and is an active maintainer for CNCF Sandbox project, "KubeArmor" and the lead author for IoT simulation framework "Whitefield". He co founded Accuknox where they are enabling runtime, zero-trust security by leveraging Linux-LSM/eBPF based security for k8s and containerized/cloud-native workloads.​Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Connecting to community and engaging at events, meeting new people, talking about cloud native technologies is something that I cherish. It is always great to be an ambassador of the community whose motto and modus operandi is something that I believe in. One specific goal that I would like to achieve is to establish a security focussed community locally by understanding activities that were undertaken at CNCF wide initiatives.

person.name: Victor Peula
person.bio: I work in cloud and platform engineering, focusing on reliability, automation, and systems designed with long-term thinking. Experience across research, startups, and regulated environments shapes how I approach resilience, security, and operational clarity. I’m drawn to open source and the CNCF ecosystem because of the engineering mindset behind them. The Kubestronaut journey reflects my commitment to continuous growth. I care about building infrastructure that teams can rely on. Good systems earn trust over time. Beyond technology, I try to leave room for curiosity, family, and the parts of life that don’t run on infrastructure.

person.name: Ahmed Mounani
person.bio: DevOps & SRE Engineer passionate about automation, containerization, orchestration, CI/CD, and cloud-native technologies. Enthusiastic about CNCF tools, I focus on optimizing production environments for scalability and resilience.

person.name: Juan Camilo Restrepo Veintemilla
person.bio: Senior Technical Support Engineer in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and cloud‑native platforms. With over 15 years of experience supporting enterprise workloads. Focused on operational excellence, and translating complex customer cases into measurable impact through mentoring and reusable best practices. Passionate about sharing knowledge through internal documentation, workshops, and community collaboration.

person.name: Néstor Alvarado Moreno

I enjoy breaking down complex platforms, working hands-on with modern architectures, and turning chaos into clarity. Outside of tech, I really enjoy eating and I’m always looking for new places to try, so feel free to send food recommendations my way!

I am truly passionate about creating platforms that developers not only embrace but also appreciate and actively support. My deep expertise in cloud-native applications and public cloud solutions informs my mentoring and consulting approach, ensuring that clients receive tailored, high-performance environments and platforms that meet their specific requirements.

person.name: Tabish Athar
person.bio: I'm working as a DevOps and SRE Professional with 8 years of experience and with a passion for exploring new technologies and taking on challenges. I bring a wealth of expertise in cloud infrastructure, especially focused on GCP, as well as AWS and Azure. I have expertise in Kubernetes, OpenShift, Cloud Native Development, Microservices, Operators, CI/CD, Observability, Infrastructure, Security, Data. When I’m not optimizing cloud infrastructures, you can find me doing research and investment in the Stock Market and collecting Sneakers. Let’s connect and explore how we can collaborate on exciting projects!

person.name: Uiseong Yu
person.bio: Hello, I'm Uiseong Yu, currently working as a Site Reliability Engineer at Kbank. In my role, I focus on ensuring our systems are stable and performant. I'm genuinely motivated by the opportunity to continuously tackle new challenges; it's an aspect of the work I really enjoy. This drive is paired with a strong commitment to efficiency and rationality – and I think that gives you a good picture for now.

person.name: Edgaras Apsega
person.bio: I am a passionate technologist with a focus on cloud-native technologies, particularly Kubernetes. As a co-organizer of Cloud Native Lithuania Meetup, I have helped to build a strong community of like-minded professionals where everyone is sharing their own experience. In addition to my meetup involvement, I am a speaker at technology conferences and currently work as an SRE at Vinted, where I bring my expertise in Kubernetes to help ensure the reliability and scalability of our systems.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because it enables me to connect with like-minded other ambassadors, share our knowledges and expertise and will help to educate and promote cloud-native technologies to a wider audience. The role also provides access to exclusive events and resources, as well as opportunities for professional growth and career development. Overall, being a Cloud Native Foundation Ambassador is a great way to give back to the community!

person.name: Gary Tong
person.bio: Gary is a seasoned Multi-Cloud Architect, demonstrating proficiency across Azure, AWS, GCP, and VMware platforms. In his role as a forward-thinking DevOps leader, he spearheads pioneering projects in containerization and infrastructure evolution. Gary is recognized for his exceptional problem-solving abilities in demanding business contexts. Furthermore, he serves as a dedicated Kubernetes instructor and influential author on LinkedIn, delivering engaging educational sessions and insightful articles.

person.name: Lukasz Gryglicki
person.bio:
Lukasz has been a Senior Developer at the CNCF since 2017 and has been working for The Linux Foundation since 2018. He is the creator and maintainer of the DevStats reporting tool. Prior to joining the CNCF, Lukasz worked remotely for companies based in the US, including Cleverstep, Jamis and Spreemo Health.

He loves to travel in polar areas, such as polar Norway, Finland and Russia. From 2011 to 2012, he was a scientist for a Polish polar expedition to the Hornsund fjord on the island of Spitsbergen, in northern Norway. Lukasz graduated from Warsaw University of Technology with a Master of Science in Engineering. He lives in a small town in Poland with his wife and two kids.

person.name: Vegard Valvik

My work involves developing efficient backend systems and implementing advanced DevOps practices to streamline and secure deployment pipelines. Passionate about programming technologies and DevOps, I continuously seek to enhance software development and operational processes.

person.name: Jack Kelly

With an interest in software delivery and nix it was natural for Jack to develop strong opinions around what constitutes a Secure Software-Supply Chain, and consults on such topics, in addition to facilitating as part of the In-Toto Steering Committee (ITSC).

He has also joined QEMU, OpenStack, Kubernetes, and more OSS projects and contributed to them. Now he is leading a support team of Linux-based products for Fujitsu's customers.

Tony enjoys learning new things and has earned certifications from AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, ISC2 CSSLP, and CNCF/Linux Foundation Kubernetes. He is now learning about Machine Learning and Generative AI, with the goal of using AI to enhance the security of cloud and DevOps platforms.
